channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Fryer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Alexa+ – Here’s how to sign up for early access
item.description: Amazon's now letting customers in the United States sign up for early access to Alexa+. Here's how to do just that and the Echo Show device you'll need to be eligible.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Monster Hunter Wilds best controller settings
item.description: Here's the best Monster Hunter Wilds controller settings, including ways to improve Focus Mode, and the handling of your Seikret.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The Rings of Power season 3 adds Stranger Things' Jamie Campbell Bower and Heartstopper's Eddie Marsan to its cast, and I think they're perfect for two specific roles
item.description: The Rings of Power season 3's cats just got a big cast boost with the addition of two talented actors.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Gabbard decries Britain's reported demand for Apple to provide backdoor access to users' cloud data
item.description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard says she has serious concerns about the British government’s reported demand that Apple provide backdoor access to any data stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Long-dormant Mac malware returns with advanced capabilities
item.description: Just two months into 2025 there have been numerous Mac malware threats targeting Apple laptops, ranging from infostealers to malicious software.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: F5 targets AI application security with renewed workload management software
item.description: F5 is evolving its core application and load balancing software to help customers secure and manage AI-powered and multicloud workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Vietnam plans energy shift toward building more solar, less reliance on gas and coal
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam is revising its energy plans to focus more on large solar farms and less on reliance on coal and natural gas. The fast-growing economy now aims to get 16% of its energy from the sun — more than triple its earlier target of just 5%.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Navy comptroller shares how financial management can successfully impact decision-making
item.description: Modern financial systems offer the ideal way to tie budget to strategy and improve decision-making, explains Russ Rumbaugh, who left the Navy in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon's new AI-powered Alexa promises to be your 'best friend in a digital world' for a monthly fee
item.description: Amazon on Wednesday unveiled a generative-AI infused Alexa that it says will allow the popular voice assistant to have more personality, check a user’s tone and even plan romantic dates.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero And Wyatte Grantham-Philips, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Red Hat OpenShift 4.18 expands cloud-native networking
item.description: Red Hat is updating its OpenShift platform with a series of capabilities that will provide more advanced networking and virtualization functionality for cloud-native deployments. OpenShift is Red Hat’s commercially supported Kubernetes distribution.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A must-try Android email upgrade
item.description: Ahem: I have two separate confessions to make, both equally embarrassing. Confession #1 is that I love email. I’m totally not kidding, either.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Red Hat OpenShift improves virtualization support
item.description: Red Hat OpenShift 4.18, the latest version of the company’s Kubernetes-powered application platform, has arrived, with enhancements designed to simplify management of virtual machines and containers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Get started with async in Python
item.description: Asynchronous programming, or async, is a feature of many modern languages that allows a program to juggle multiple operations without waiting or getting hung up on any one of them.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 11 rules for writing better code
item.description: You can’t develop software and manage software projects for 30 years and not learn a thing or two or twelve about writing good code. Here are a near-dozen nuggets of coding wisdom I picked up along the way.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI, automation spur efforts to upskill network pros
item.description: Networking skills are evolving to keep pace with broader technology trends, and network pros need to proactively develop new competencies in areas such as AI and automation to stay relevant.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cot framework aims to ease Rust web development
item.description: Web developers looking for an easier time with the Rust language may want to check out Cot, an emerging web framework pitched as being for “lazy developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Reported cuts at NIST imperil semiconductor reshoring plans in the US
item.description: Proposed cuts at a key US government agency will likely mean the demise of CHIPS Act funding, which could effectively end the program and undermine efforts to reshore semiconductor manufacturing and research to the US.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Atlassian refocuses Trello on individual task management
item.description: Atlassian’s Trello project management tool has received a facelift with features designed to organize meetings and ideas in a cohesive manner.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco, Nvidia expand AI partnership to include Silicon One technology
item.description: Cisco and Nvidia have expanded their collaboration to support enterprise AI implementations by tying together Cisco’s Silicon One technology and Nvidia’s Ethernet networking platform.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: IBM Acquires DataStax to Boost Watsonx's Generative AI Capabilities
item.description: IBM acquires DataStax to boost watsonx's generative AI capabilities and enhance its portfolio of products for accelerating the use of generative AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: IBM targets AI application growth with DataStax buy
item.description: Looking to bolster its AI data handling and storage capabilities, IBM said it had entered an agreement to buy open-source database developer DataStax for an undisclosed amount.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Precise asset management leads to impactful zero trust initiatives
item.description: Agencies’ attention and efforts are now shifting towards strengthening cybersecurity defenses to address the more challenging aspects of cyber threats. The post Precise asset management leads to impactful zero trust initiatives first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: I’m a tech expert. These are the 5 time-saving tech tips I tell everyone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson presents five secret shortcuts: Adjust sound/vibration, share YouTube moments, fix tabs and tweak brightness.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Embracing a Cloud-Smart Approach: Lessons from Government IT Strategies
item.description: Discover how government agencies can learn from private sector approaches to cloud computing and adopt a more strategic, cost-effective strategy.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: VA EHR office shrinks headcount under Trump’s federal workforce policies
item.description: VA officials are working on an updated EHR deployment schedule beyond the Michigan go-lives in mid-2026. The post VA EHR office shrinks headcount under Trump’s federal workforce policies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: AI & Data Exchange 2025: Air Force’s Anthony Genatempo, Alexis Bonnell on scaling AI adoption for acquisition
item.description: The Air Force PEO for Cyber and Networks and the Air Force Research Lab collaborated on AI tools to reduce burden and time in the acquisition process.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Protecting US supply chains from foreign influence
item.description: With the new Trump administration likely to place an even greater focus on reducing reliance on China, this trend is expected to accelerate. The post Protecting US supply chains from foreign influence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4
